reasoning: The baseline expectation for a stock's 1-week move is near 50%. Because the resolution compares unadjusted closing prices between March 13 (effectively March 15) and March 20 (effectively March 22), the stock will experience a mechanical drop of ~$0.10 relative to the starting price of $16.88. This represents an expected headwind of about -0.6%. Given the standard weekly volatility of a REIT like DOC (approx. 2.5-3.0%, or ~$0.45), an expected drop of $0.10 shifts the normal distribution z-score to approximately -0.20, which translates to roughly a 41-42% probability that the final price will be higher than the starting price. No other major immediate catalysts such as earnings or the expected Janus Living IPO are definitively scheduled for this specific 5-day trading window to offset this baseline disadvantage.
